content.problemStatement:
After acquiring an extraordinary amount of knowledge through programming contests, Malvika decided to harness her expertise to train the next generation of Indian programmers. So, she decided to hold a programming camp. In the camp, she held a discussion session for **n** members (**n-1** students, and herself). They are sitting in a line from left to right numbered through **1** to **n**.

Malvika is sitting in the **n**th spot. She wants to teach **m** topics of competitive programming to the students. As the people taking part in the camp are all newbies, they know none of the topics being taught, i.e., initially, the first **n - 1** people in the line know none of the topics, while the **n**th knows all of them.

It takes one hour for a person to learn a topic from his neighbor. Obviously, one person cannot both teach a topic as well as learn one during the same hour. That is, in any particular hour, a person can either teach a topic that he knows to one of his neighbors, or he can learn a topic from one of his neighbors, or he can sit idly. It is also obvious that if person x is learning from person y at a particular hour, then person y must be teaching person x at that hour.

Also, note that people can work parallely too, i.e., in the same hour when the 4th person is teaching the 3rd person, the 1st person can also teach the 2nd or learn from 2nd.

Find out the minimum number of hours needed so that each person learns all the **m** topics.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer **T** denoting the number of test cases.
The only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers **n, m** as defined in the statement.


Output Format

For each test case, output a single integer in a line corresponding to the answer of the problem.
content.inputFormat: The first line of input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers n, m as defined in the statement.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer in a line corresponding to the answer of the problem.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, there are two people and one topic. The second person teaches the first person, which takes one hour. For the second test case, there are three people and two topics. The second person learns the first topic from the third person in the first hour, then teaches it to the first person in the second hour. The third person teaches the second topic to the second person in the third hour, and the second person teaches it to the first person in the fourth hour.

solution.approach: To find the minimum number of hours needed for all people to learn all m topics, we can observe a pattern. The first person needs to learn m topics from the second person, which takes m hours. Then, the second person teaches the first person, and the third person learns from the second person in the next hour, and so on. This process continues until all people have learned all topics. The total time taken can be calculated using the formula: 2*m - 1 for n = 2, and m + (n - 2) * ceil(m / 2) for n > 2.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) — The time complexity is linear because we are simply iterating over each test case and performing a constant amount of work for each case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — The space complexity is constant because we are using a fixed amount of space to store the input and output values.
